description: Configure OAuth2/SSO identity providers to enable single sign-on login for platform users

actors.name: Platform Administrator
actors.description: Configures identity provider settings and manages OAuth service integrations

actors.name: User
actors.description: Authenticates via an external identity provider instead of local credentials

actors.name: Identity Provider
actors.description: External OAuth2/OpenID Connect service that authenticates the user and issues tokens

actors.name: Authentication Service
actors.description: Platform component that handles OAuth flow, token exchange, and user record merging

general: Each OAuth provider must have a unique service_name; re-configuring the same name updates the existing provider
server_url must be a valid absolute URL; relative paths are resolved against it for token_path and identity_path
client_secret must be stored encrypted at rest and never exposed in API responses or logs
If merge_users is enabled, the platform matches incoming identities to existing accounts by email before creating a new user
Field mappings (username_field, email_field, name_field, avatar_field) override platform defaults when provided
The roles_claim field, if present, maps identity provider roles to platform roles
On successful authentication the platform creates or updates the user record and sets the provider service on the user identity
Access tokens obtained via OAuth are registered as valid session tokens within the platform
Administrators may enable or disable each provider independently without affecting other providers
Users authenticated via SSO retain a link to their identity provider service; password login remains disabled for pure-SSO accounts unless explicitly enabled
Invalid or expired tokens from the identity provider must result in session termination on the platform

given: user initiates login via a configured OAuth provider
identity provider returns a valid authorization code
platform successfully exchanges the code for tokens and retrieves the user identity
user_authenticated.result: User is logged in; a session is created and the identity provider service is linked to the user record

given: user authenticates via OAuth for the first time
no existing platform account matches the identity
new_user_provisioned.result: A new user account is created from the identity provider profile data

given: an existing platform account shares the same email as the identity
existing_user_merged.result: Incoming OAuth identity is linked to the existing account; the user continues with their existing profile

given: administrator submits valid OAuth provider configuration
provider_configured.result: Provider is saved and the login button for this service becomes available on the login screen

given: platform attempts to exchange authorization code for access token
identity provider returns an error or invalid response
token_exchange_failed.result: Login attempt is aborted and the user is shown a generic authentication error

given: platform successfully obtains an access token
identity endpoint returns an error or missing required fields
identity_fetch_failed.result: Login is rejected; user is informed that profile information could not be retrieved

given: administrator saves a provider configuration with an invalid server_url or missing client_id
provider_misconfigured.result: Configuration is rejected with a validation error; provider is not activated

errors.message: Authentication failed. The identity provider did not return a valid token. Please try again.
Authentication failed. Your profile information could not be retrieved from the identity provider.
The identity provider configuration is invalid. Please check the settings and try again.

events.name: oauth.login_success
events.description: Fires when a user successfully authenticates via an OAuth provider

events.name: oauth.login_failed
events.description: Fires when an OAuth login attempt fails at any stage of the flow

events.name: oauth.user_provisioned
events.description: Fires when a new user account is created from an OAuth identity

events.name: oauth.user_merged
events.description: Fires when an OAuth identity is linked to an existing user account

events.name: oauth.provider_configured
events.description: Fires when an administrator saves a new or updated OAuth provider configuration

related.reason: OAuth SSO is an authentication method that integrates into the platform login flow
SSO-provisioned users may bypass manual registration depending on configuration
Roles from the identity provider's roles_claim can be mapped to platform roles
Successful OAuth authentication creates a managed session

goals.description: Enable users to authenticate via external OAuth2/OpenID Connect providers with secure token handling and identity merging

success_metrics.measurement: API responses or logs containing client_secret / total responses
Successful OAuth logins / total OAuth login attempts

human_checkpoints: before enabling merge_users_distinct_services across multiple identity providers
before disabling an OAuth provider that is the only login method for some users

invariants: client_secret is stored encrypted and never returned in API responses or logs
invalid or expired identity provider tokens result in session termination
SSO-only accounts do not have password login enabled unless explicitly configured

acceptance_tests.given: identity provider returns an error during code exchange
acceptance_tests.expect: login aborted with OAUTH_TOKEN_EXCHANGE_FAILED and generic error shown to user

acceptance_tests.scenario: existing user merged on email match
acceptance_tests.given: merge_users is true and OAuth identity email matches an existing account
acceptance_tests.expect: OAuth identity linked to existing account rather than creating a new user

capabilities.description: Orchestrate OAuth2 authorization code flow including token exchange and identity fetch
Match incoming OAuth identities to existing accounts by email and link services

tradeoffs.reason: client secrets are never exposed even in admin UIs to prevent credential leakage
